title: anthemis pseudoabrotanifolia
intro: Anthemis pseudoabrotanifolia, also known as False Horehound, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and calm digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for a range of ailments. Anthemis pseudoabrotanifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of illness.
